methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $7.5B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Skydio
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.2B

data.narrative:
Flock Safety, a Series E defense and security company, was last valued at $7.5B post-money after raising $500M+ to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 10 companies across Defense & Security—two public comparables (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and eight private peers including Saronic, Shield AI, Chaos Industries, Skydio, and Epirus.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.4x step-up multiplier from Series E to the next financing stage, implying a valuation of $10.5B. Peer-median analysis examined five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Saronic at $9.2B, Shield AI at $5.6B, Chaos Industries at $4.5B, Skydio at $2.2B, and Epirus at $1.4B), yielding a median valuation of $4.5B with a range of $1.4B to $9.2B.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $3.8B (low, representing downside toward peer median) to $12.1B (high, stage-progression plus upside buffer), with a median of $7.5B anchored to the last post-money valuation. This suggests the current $7.5B valuation sits at the median of the implied range. Key uncertainties include heterogeneity in peer stage progression and the degree to which Flock Safety's market position commands a premium or discount to the median peer set.
